name: Handmade English Muffins
recipeIngredient: 4 cups All-purpose flour, whole wheat, or both
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and stir in 1 tablespoon of sugar, salt, and vegetable oil. Pour into a large bowl and let stand until tepid.
2. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of sugar and 1 tablespoon of yeast into the lukewarm water. Let stand for 10 minutes.
3. Stir the yeast mixture into the milk mixture. Beat in egg (if using) and add 2 cups of flour until smooth. Cover and let rise until doubled and bubbly (~1.25 hours).
4. Gently push down on the bubbles and add the remaining 2 cups of flour. Knead on a floured surface, adding more flour as needed.
5. Let the dough rest until doubled in size. Sprinkle cornmeal on a clean surface and roll the dough to about 1/2 inch thick.
6. Sprinkle cornmeal on top of the rolled dough. Use a cup or biscuit cutter to cut into rounds.
7. Cover and let rise again until doubled in size.
8. Heat an electric skillet to medium (300°F). Spray with Pam or lightly grease the pan.
9. Place muffins on the hot surface, cover the pan, and cook for 3-8 minutes on one side until browned.
10. Flip the muffins and cook for another 3-8 minutes on the other side.
Yield: 18-20
